text.title: Requirements Traceability — Examples
text.summary: E-commerce checkout traceability tree, impact analysis table, tools, and antipatterns.

example.note: Business Requirement BR-05: Enable customers to complete purchases online.
SR-12: Customer can checkout cart (satisfies BR-05).
FR-30: Display cart summary (derives SR-12) → D-15: Cart component design (implements FR-30) → TC-45: Verify cart displays correctly (verifies FR-30).
FR-31: Accept payment information (derives SR-12) → D-16: Payment form design (implements FR-31) → TC-46: Verify payment entry (verifies FR-31).
FR-32: Confirm order (derives SR-12) → D-17: Confirmation page design (implements FR-32) → TC-47: Verify order confirmation (verifies FR-32).
Coverage: BR-05 is fully covered (forward) and every test cites its FR (backward). Orphan count: 0.
Scenario: Business requirement BR-03 is changing. Use the link graph to identify all affected artifacts within 2 hops.
Stakeholder Requirements: SR-05, SR-06 — needs review.
Solution Requirements: FR-10, FR-11, FR-12 — may need update.
Design Documents: D-08, D-09 — needs revision.
Test Cases: TC-20, TC-21, TC-22 — needs update.
Human-in-the-loop gate: impact sign-off required before any artifact is modified.

list.item: Requirements Management — Jira, Azure DevOps, Jama Connect: best for KA-3 traceability matrices via issue links.
Specialized RM — IBM DOORS Next, Polarion ALM, StrictDoc: required for regulated industries (ISO 13485, IEC 62304, DO-178C).
Git-native RM — Doorstop: Markdown + YAML with built-in link and publish commands.
Spreadsheets — Excel, Google Sheets: acceptable for small projects only; go stale without a generator.
Generator script — rtm.py: aggregates frontmatter traces across a docs tree into a coverage report.

antipattern.description: Hand-editing the RTM instead of patching source artifact frontmatter.
antipattern.reason: Manual RTM edits bypass the generator and produce inconsistencies; the matrix must be a build product, not a hand-maintained document.

antipattern.description: One-time traceability exercise — not updating on changes.
antipattern.reason: An outdated RTM is worse than none because it gives false assurance to auditors and misleads impact analysis.

antipattern.description: Coverage metrics gaming — linking one tautological test per requirement to achieve 100% forward coverage.
antipattern.reason: Tautological links pass coverage thresholds while hiding real gaps; pair coverage metrics with mutation testing or acceptance-criteria density to detect gaming.
